quiz_metadata.title: One-Way ANOVA Pre-Test
quiz_metadata.description: Answer the following questions based on your current understanding. You are not expected to have prior knowledge of One-Way ANOVA. Answer to the best of your ability.

shared_stimulus.text: A researcher is conducting a comparative statistical study to evaluate whether mean exam scores differ significantly across four distinct teaching methods. The study includes a total sample of 200 students, with 50 students randomly assigned to each instructional method to ensure balanced group sizes. After a standardized final exam scored on a 100-point scale, the average scores were as follows: Method A (traditional lecture) had a mean score of 78.4 (SD = 8.6), Method B (interactive discussion) had a mean of 82.1 (SD = 7.9), Method C (blended learning) produced a mean of 85.3 (SD = 6.8), and Method D (fully online instruction) had a mean of 76.9 (SD = 9.4). To determine whether these observed differences in group means are statistically significant, the researcher plans to conduct a statistical test to analyze their results.

questions.title: When to Use One-Way ANOVA
questions.question_text: Which statistical method is most appropriate?

questions.title: Variables and Structure
questions.question_text: In a One-Way ANOVA, which of the following correctly describes the variables involved?

answers.text: One continuous explanatory variable and one categorical response variable
One categorical explanatory variable with two or more levels and one continuous response variable

questions.title: Hypotheses
questions.question_text: In words, what is the null hypothesis in a One-Way ANOVA?
questions.grading_note: Manual grading required. Expected answer: The null hypothesis states that the population means of all groups are equal (i.e., there is no difference in mean exam scores across the four teaching methods).

questions.title: Variability
questions.question_text: The F-statistic in a One-Way ANOVA is based on the comparison between:

questions.title: Assumptions
questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a standard assumption of One-Way ANOVA?

answers.text: The response variable is normally distributed within each group
The explanatory variable must be normally distributed

questions.title: Interpretation
questions.question_text: Suppose a One-Way ANOVA produces a very small p-value (i.e., 0.00023). What does this suggest about the group means?
questions.grading_note: Manual grading required. Expected answer: A very small p-value provides strong evidence against the null hypothesis, suggesting that at least one group mean is significantly different from the others (i.e., teaching method has a statistically significant effect on exam scores).
